ogTitle: Lake Gairdner vs Lake Macfarlane: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lake Gairdner and Lake Macfarlane.
heading: Lake Gairdner vs Lake Macfarlane — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lake Gairdner, South Australia and Lake Macfarlane, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lake Macfarlane has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Lake Gairdner (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lake Gairdner or Lake Macfarlane safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lake Gairdner has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Lake Macfarlane scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lake Gairdner scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Lake Macfarlane scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lake Gairdner or Lake Macfarlane?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lake Macfarlane has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
